name: crm_short_circuit_inherited
description:
Pins the CRM overlay short-circuit at engine/assessor.py:733-746 — the SECOND deterministic short-circuit (after rule #8), gated on every specified responsibility being in _CRM_SHORT_CIRCUIT_SET = frozenset({provider, inherited, not_applicable}) at assessor.py:468. With responsibility='inherited' and no responsibility_onprem, specified=['inherited'], `all(r in _CRM_SHORT_CIRCUIT_SET ...)` is True, and the kernel returns via _finalize_crm_decision (assessor.py:1833) WITHOUT consulting the LLM — stub.calls stays empty.

Distinct from dual_narrative_crm_customer_mismatch.json (which uses the SAME runner crm_entries plumbing but selects responsibility='customer' — a value NOT in _CRM_SHORT_CIRCUIT_SET — so the row falls through to the LLM). This case is the symmetric demonstration: an inheritable responsibility that DOES short-circuit, proving the rule-1.5 gate at assessor.py:738-742 fires when the case file declares one.

_finalize_crm_decision maps responsibility='inherited' → status=COMPLIANT + narrative_class=COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING via status_map at assessor.py:1858-1862, then takes the CRM-supplied narrative verbatim (cloud_narr_text = entry.narrative at :1899) since the customer's CRM text is authoritative — no validator runs (assessor.py:1847: 'Validator is skipped intentionally: the verdict comes from an authoritative external document'). source='crm_inherited' is composed at assessor.py:2019 as f'crm_{cloud_r or "na"}' with no on-prem suffix because responsibility_onprem is None.

llm_stub_proposals=[] is load-bearing — if the kernel regressed and reached the LLM, the empty queue would raise IndexError from StubLlmClient.propose_twice, surfacing the regression loudly rather than silently consulting a model. llm_calls=0 pins the patent-aligned 'authoritative document wins' invariant: when the customer has formally signed off on inheritance via the CRM, the assessor must NOT re-litigate via the LLM.

tagged_evidence is intentionally non-empty (a generic Account Management Plan reference) to prove the short-circuit fires REGARDLESS of evidence — the CRM determination doesn't depend on whether evidence exists; an inherited control is inherited even when the customer also happens to have local artifacts. This is distinct from rule_no_evidence which short-circuits ONLY when evidence is absent.

Control ID 'ac-2' (OSCAL canonical lowercase) matches what _lookup_crm at assessor.py:1830 constructs via _ccis_to_oscal_control_id(_normalize_control('AC-2')); source_baseline_id=999 is the sentinel value already used by dual_narrative_crm_customer_mismatch — both eval cases route through the same CRM overlay path so consistency keeps the failure messages diff-able.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization defines and documents the account types permitted within the information system.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine documented account management plan; confirm enumerated account types approved by designated approval authority.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00099999 Account Management Plan Rev D — local privileged-account enrollment procedures.

crm_entries.narrative: Account-type definition for the customer-managed tenant is inherited from the FedRAMP-authorized AWS GovCloud authorization boundary documented in section 4.2 of the provider package.
